Study Title: KeyLargo: Pembrolizumab + Oxaliplatin + Capecitabine in Gastric Cancer
Trial Summary: The KeyLargo trial (NCT03342937) is a completed Phase 2 study that evaluated the safety and efficacy of combining pembrolizumab with oxaliplatin and capecitabine in patients with previously untreated metastatic esophagogastric adenocarcinoma. The trial involved a safety validation cohort followed by a dose expansion cohort, aiming to assess progression-free survival with this combination therapy. This study is relevant to the gastric cancer landscape as it explores the potential of pembrolizumab, an immunotherapy, in combination with chemotherapy, offering insights into novel treatment strategies for this patient population.

Study Title: Pembrolizumab and Palliative Radiation Therapy in Treating Patients With Metastatic Esophagus, Stomach, or Gastroesophageal Junction Cancer
Indication/Condition: Esophagus cancer, stomach cancer, gastroesophageal junction cancer
Trial Summary: This completed phase II trial evaluated the efficacy of combining pembrolizumab with palliative radiation therapy in patients with metastatic esophagus, stomach, or gastroesophageal junction cancer. The study is relevant to the gastric cancer landscape as it explores the potential synergistic effects of immunotherapy and radiation therapy, offering insights into novel treatment strategies for advanced-stage cancers. Despite the small sample size, the trial contributes valuable data on the integration of monoclonal antibodies in palliative care settings.

Study Title: Trastuzumab in Combination With Capecitabine and Oxaliplatin(XELOX) in Patients With Advanced Gastric Cancer(AGC): Her+XELOX
Indication/Condition: adenocarcinoma of the stomach, gastro-oesophageal junction
Trial Summary: The Her+XELOX trial was a phase II study that assessed the efficacy and safety of combining trastuzumab with capecitabine and oxaliplatin as a first-line treatment for patients with HER2 positive advanced gastric cancer. With an enrollment of 55 participants, this trial is significant as it explores the potential benefits of targeting HER2 in gastric cancer, a novel approach that could improve outcomes for this patient population.

Study Title: A Study of Herceptin (Trastuzumab) in Patients With Metastatic or Advanced Gastric Cancer With Disease Progression
Trial Summary: This Phase 2 trial, NCT02005484, evaluated the efficacy and safety of Herceptin (Trastuzumab) monotherapy in patients with metastatic or advanced gastric cancer who experienced disease progression during platinum-based or 5-fluoropyrimidine-based chemotherapy. Although the trial was terminated with only 6 participants enrolled, it remains of interest as it explored the potential of Herceptin, a targeted therapy, in a challenging patient population, contributing to the understanding of treatment options for advanced gastric cancer.

Study Title: Tislelizumab in Combination With Chemotherapy as First-Line Treatment in Adults With Inoperable, Locally Advanced or Metastatic Gastric, or Gastroesophageal Junction Carcinoma
Trial Summary: The completed Phase 3 trial evaluated the efficacy and safety of tislelizumab combined with chemotherapy compared to a placebo with chemotherapy in 997 adults with inoperable, locally advanced, or metastatic gastric or gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma. This large-scale study is significant for the gastric cancer landscape as it explores the potential of tislelizumab, an immune checkpoint inhibitor, to improve first-line treatment outcomes, offering insights into novel therapeutic strategies for this challenging cancer type.
Endpoints: Progression-free Survival: median PFS 7.2 mo vs 5.9 mo, HR 0.68 (95% CI 0.56‑0.83)

Study Title: Ipilimumab or FOLFOX in Combination With Nivolumab and Trastuzumab in HER2 Positive EsophagoGastric Adenocarcinoma
Trial Summary: The INTEGA study, a Phase 2 trial with 97 participants, evaluated the efficacy of two first-line treatment strategies for HER2 positive esophagogastric adenocarcinoma: a chemo-free regimen combining Trastuzumab, Nivolumab, and Ipilimumab, and the addition of Nivolumab to the standard FOLFOX chemotherapy with Trastuzumab. This trial is significant for the gastric cancer landscape as it explores the potential of immunotherapy combinations to improve survival outcomes, offering insights into alternative treatment strategies beyond traditional chemotherapy.

Study Title: DS-8201a in HER2-positive Gastric Cancer That Cannot Be Surgically Removed or Has Spread (DESTINY-Gastric02)
Trial Summary: The DESTINY-Gastric02 trial is a Phase 2 study evaluating the safety and efficacy of trastuzumab deruxtecan in 79 participants with HER2-positive gastric or gastroesophageal junction cancer that is inoperable or metastatic and has progressed following trastuzumab treatment. This trial is significant as it explores a novel therapeutic option for a challenging patient population with limited treatment alternatives, potentially offering new hope for improved outcomes in HER2-positive gastric cancer.

Study Title: Trastuzumab Plus Docetaxel and Capecitabine For First Line Treatment of Her2-Positive Advanced Gastric Cancer
Trial Summary: This Phase 2 trial evaluated the efficacy and safety of Trastuzumab combined with Capecitabine and Docetaxel as a first-line treatment for HER2-positive advanced gastric cancer in Chinese patients. With an enrollment of 67 participants, the study aimed to provide insights into progression-free survival (PFS) with a target of 6.7 months. The trial is relevant to the gastric cancer landscape as it explores a targeted therapy approach for a specific genetic marker (HER2), which could lead to more personalized and effective treatment options for patients with this aggressive cancer type.

Study Title: Epirubicin Hydrochloride, Cisplatin, and Fluorouracil or Capecitabine With or Without Lapatinib Ditosylate as First-Line Therapy in Treating Patients With Stomach Cancer or Gastroesophageal Junction Cancer
Trial Summary: This phase II trial, NCT01123473, investigated the efficacy of combining epirubicin hydrochloride, cisplatin, and fluorouracil or capecitabine with or without lapatinib ditosylate as a first-line treatment for stomach or gastroesophageal junction cancer. Although the trial was terminated with an enrollment of 29 participants, it remains of interest due to its exploration of lapatinib ditosylate, a novel agent targeting specific enzymes involved in tumor growth, which could provide insights into future therapeutic strategies for gastric cancer.

Study Title: Phase II Study of Ramucirumab With Chemotherapy in Patients With Metastatic Gastroesophageal Junction and Gastric Cancer
Trial Summary: This Phase II trial (NCT02726399) aimed to evaluate the effects of adding ramucirumab to the standard treatment of trastuzumab and chemotherapy in patients with metastatic gastroesophageal junction and gastric cancer. Although the trial was terminated with only 7 participants enrolled, it remains of interest due to its exploration of ramucirumab as a potential enhancement to existing treatment regimens, which could inform future research directions in the gastric cancer therapeutic landscape.

Study Title: A Study to Compare Zolbetuximab (IMAB362) and Chemotherapy With Placebo and Chemotherapy in Adults With Gastric Cancer.
Trial Summary: This Phase 3 trial investigates the efficacy of zolbetuximab combined with chemotherapy versus placebo with chemotherapy in adults with advanced gastric or gastroesophageal junction cancer, focusing on tumors expressing the Claudin 18.2 protein. With an enrollment of 565 participants, the study is significant due to its exploration of a novel target, Claudin 18.2, which could offer a new therapeutic avenue for patients with limited treatment options. The trial's large sample size and double-blind design enhance its potential impact on the gastric cancer treatment landscape.

Study Title: A Study of Pembrolizumab (MK-3475) in Participants With Recurrent or Metastatic Gastric or Gastroesophageal Junction Adenocarcinoma (MK-3475-059/KEYNOTE-059)
Trial Summary: The KEYNOTE-059 trial investigated the efficacy of pembrolizumab, both as a monotherapy and in combination with chemotherapy, in participants with recurrent or metastatic gastric or gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma. Enrolling 318 participants, this Phase 2 study aimed to assess the Overall Response Rate (ORR) of pembrolizumab, offering insights into its potential as a treatment option for advanced gastric cancer. This trial is significant for the gastric cancer landscape as it explores the use of an immune checkpoint inhibitor, pembrolizumab, which represents a novel therapeutic approach in this patient population.
Endpoints: Overall survival: median OS 5.5 mo (95% CI 4.2‑6.7) for all participants, 5.8 mo (95% CI 4.4‑7.8) for PD-L1 positive participants
Endpoints: Progression-free Survival: median PFS 2.0 mo (95% CI 2.0‑2.0) for all participants, 2.1 mo (95% CI 2.0‑2.1) for PD-L1 positive participants

Study Title: Efficacy and Safety of Pembrolizumab (MK-3475) Plus Lenvatinib (E7080/MK-7902) in Previously Treated Participants With Select Solid Tumors (MK-7902-005/E7080-G000-224/LEAP-005)
Indication/Condition: triple negative breast cancer, ovarian cancer, gastric cancer, colorectal cancer, glioblastoma, biliary tract cancers, pancreatic cancer
Trial Summary: The trial investigated the efficacy and safety of pembrolizumab combined with lenvatinib in previously treated patients with various solid tumors, including gastric cancer. With an enrollment of 603 participants, this Phase 2 study provides valuable insights into the potential benefits of this combination therapy for gastric cancer, highlighting its relevance due to the novel approach of combining an immune checkpoint inhibitor with a tyrosine kinase inhibitor, which could offer new therapeutic options for patients with limited treatment alternatives.

Study Title: PANGEA-IMBBP: Personalized Antibodies for Gastro-Esophageal Adenocarcinoma - A 1st Pilot Metastatic Trial of Biologics Beyond Progression
Intervention(s): Trastuzumab vs ABT-806 vs Bemarituzumab vs Ramucirumab vs Nivolumab
Trial Summary: The PANGEA-IMBBP trial is a Phase 2 study that enrolled 80 participants to evaluate the efficacy of personalized antibody treatments for metastatic gastro-esophageal adenocarcinoma, using molecular profiling to tailor therapies such as Trastuzumab, ABT-806, Bemarituzumab, Ramucirumab, and Nivolumab. This trial is significant for the gastric cancer landscape as it explores the potential of precision medicine to improve treatment outcomes by targeting specific tumor abnormalities, offering insights into personalized therapeutic strategies.

Study Title: Study of Pembrolizumab (MK-3475) as First-Line Monotherapy and Combination Therapy for Treatment of Advanced Gastric or Gastroesophageal Junction Adenocarcinoma (MK-3475-062/KEYNOTE-062)
Trial Summary: The KEYNOTE-062 trial is a completed Phase 3 study evaluating pembrolizumab as a first-line treatment for advanced gastric or gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma, enrolling 763 participants. This trial is significant for the gastric cancer landscape as it explores the efficacy of pembrolizumab, an immunotherapy targeting PD-L1, both as monotherapy and in combination with standard chemotherapy, potentially offering new therapeutic options for patients with varying levels of PD-L1 expression.

Study Title: DS-8201a in Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2)-Expressing Gastric Cancer [DESTINY-Gastric01]
Trial Summary: The DESTINY-Gastric01 trial is a Phase 2 study that evaluated the efficacy and safety of DS-8201a compared to physician's choice of treatment (Irinotecan or Paclitaxel) in patients with HER2-overexpressing advanced gastric or gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma who have progressed after two prior treatment regimens. This trial is significant for the gastric cancer landscape as it explores a novel therapeutic option, DS-8201a, which could potentially offer improved outcomes for patients with limited treatment options.

Study Title: A Study of Zolbetuximab (IMAB362) Plus CAPOX Compared With Placebo Plus CAPOX as First-line Treatment of Subjects With Claudin (CLDN) 18.2-positive, HER2-negative, Locally Advanced Unresectable or Metastatic Gastric or Gastroesophageal Junction (GEJ) Adenocarcinoma (GLOW).
Trial Summary: The GLOW trial is a Phase 3 study evaluating the efficacy of zolbetuximab combined with CAPOX chemotherapy compared to a placebo in treating adults with Claudin 18.2-positive, HER2-negative, locally advanced unresectable or metastatic gastric or gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma. With an enrollment of 507 participants, this trial is significant as it explores a novel target, Claudin 18.2, which could potentially offer a new therapeutic option for patients with advanced gastric cancer, addressing an unmet medical need in regions where zolbetuximab is not yet approved.

Study Title: Phase II Trial of Pembrolizumab With Trastuzumab and Chemotherapy in Advanced HER2 Positive Esophagogastric (EG) Cancer
Trial Summary: This Phase II trial investigated the effects of pembrolizumab combined with trastuzumab and chemotherapy in patients with advanced HER2 positive esophagogastric cancer, enrolling 37 participants. The study is relevant to the gastric cancer landscape as it explores the potential benefits of combining immunotherapy with targeted therapy, which could offer new treatment avenues for this aggressive cancer type.

Study Title: A Study of Trastuzumab in Combination With TS-ONE & Cisplatin in First-line Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2)-Positive Advanced Gastric Cancer
Trial Summary: This Phase 2 trial investigates the safety and efficacy of combining Trastuzumab with TS-ONE and cisplatin as a first-line treatment for HER2-positive advanced gastric cancer, enrolling 30 participants. The study is relevant to the gastric cancer landscape as it explores a novel combination therapy targeting HER2, which could offer a new treatment avenue for patients with this specific cancer subtype.

Study Title: Planning Treatment for Oesophago-gastric Cancer: a Maintenance Therapy Trial
Trial Summary: This Phase 2 trial, with an enrollment of 494 participants, is evaluating the efficacy of maintenance therapies using MEDI4736 (Durvalumab), Rucaparib, and Ramucirumab in patients with locally advanced or metastatic HER-2 positive or negative oesophago-gastric adenocarcinomas following standard first-line chemotherapy. The trial is significant for the gastric cancer landscape as it explores novel maintenance therapy options, potentially offering new treatment avenues for a challenging cancer type.

Study Title: Explorative Phase II Study of Perioperative Treatment in Patients With Adenocarcinoma of the Gastroesophageal Junction or Stomach
Trial Summary: This completed Phase II trial investigated the efficacy of trastuzumab as a perioperative treatment in patients with adenocarcinoma of the gastroesophageal junction or stomach, enrolling 53 participants. The study aimed to assess the rate of complete pathological responses, providing valuable insights into the potential of trastuzumab in improving treatment outcomes for this patient population. This trial is relevant to the gastric cancer landscape as it explores the use of a targeted therapy, which could lead to more personalized and effective treatment strategies.

Study Title: Study of BO-112 With Pembrolizumab for Colorectal or Gastric/GEJ Cancer With Liver Metastasis
Indication/Condition: colorectal cancer, gastric cancer, gastroesophageal junction cancer
Trial Summary: The trial titled "Study of BO-112 With Pembrolizumab for Colorectal or Gastric/GEJ Cancer With Liver Metastasis" (NCT04508140) was a phase 2 study that explored the combination of intratumoral BO-112 and intravenous pembrolizumab in patients with liver metastasis from colorectal, gastric, or gastroesophageal junction cancers. Despite its termination, the trial remains of interest due to its focus on overcoming primary resistance to PD-1 inhibitors in microsatellite stable tumors, a significant challenge in the treatment of these cancers. The study's innovative approach to modifying the tumor microenvironment and its potential implications for enhancing immunotherapy efficacy highlight its relevance in the gastric-cancer landscape.

Study Title: Pembrolizumab (MK-3475) Plus Chemotherapy Versus Placebo Plus Chemotherapy in Participants Gastric or Gastroesophageal Junction (GEJ) Adenocarcinoma (MK-3475-859/KEYNOTE-859)
Trial Summary: The completed Phase 3 trial evaluated the efficacy of pembrolizumab combined with chemotherapy compared to a placebo with chemotherapy in treating HER2-negative advanced gastric or gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma in adults. With a large enrollment of 1579 participants, this study is significant for the gastric cancer landscape as it explores the potential of pembrolizumab, an immunotherapy, to improve overall survival in a challenging cancer type, potentially offering a new therapeutic option for patients.
Endpoints: Progression-free Survival: median PFS 6.9 mo vs 5.6 mo, HR 0.76 (95% CI 0.67‑0.85)

Study Title: Pre-operative Nivolumab and Ipilimumab, Followed by Post-operative Nivolumab, for MSI/dMMR Oeso-gastric Adenocarcinoma.
Trial Summary: This phase II trial investigates the efficacy of pre-operative nivolumab and ipilimumab, followed by post-operative nivolumab, in patients with MSI/dMMR localized oeso-gastric adenocarcinoma. With an enrollment of 32 participants, the study aims to assess the complete pathologic response rate of this immunotherapy combination. This trial is significant for the gastric cancer landscape as it explores the potential of immunotherapy in a specific genetic subset of gastric cancer, which could lead to more personalized and effective treatment strategies.

Study Title: Pembrolizumab and Monoclonal Antibody Therapy in Advanced Cancer
Intervention(s): Pembrolizumab vs Trastuzumab vs ado-trastuzumab emtansine vs Cetuximab
Indication/Condition: gastric cancer, GEJ cancer, metastatic breast cancer, HNSCC, metastatic CRC
Trial Summary: The trial titled "Pembrolizumab and Monoclonal Antibody Therapy in Advanced Cancer" (NCT02318901) was a Phase 1/2 study that explored the combination of pembrolizumab with various monoclonal antibodies, including trastuzumab, ado-trastuzumab emtansine, and cetuximab, in patients with advanced cancers. Despite its termination, the trial remains of interest for the gastric cancer landscape as it investigated novel combination therapies targeting HER2 overexpressing gastric or gastroesophageal junction cancers, potentially offering insights into new therapeutic strategies for these difficult-to-treat malignancies.

Study Title: A Study to Evaluate the Efficacy and Safety of Trastuzumab in Combination With Capecitabine and Oxaliplatin as First-line Chemotherapy for Inoperable, Locally Advanced or Recurrent and/or Metastatic Gastric Cancer
Trial Summary: This phase 2 trial evaluated the efficacy and safety of Trastuzumab combined with Capecitabine and Oxaliplatin as a first-line treatment for inoperable, locally advanced, or recurrent/metastatic gastric cancer in Chinese patients. With 51 participants, the study builds on previous findings from the ToGA trial, exploring a potentially more tolerable chemotherapy regimen by substituting Cisplatin with Oxaliplatin. This trial is relevant as it addresses the need for effective treatment options in HER2-positive gastric cancer, particularly in regions like China where the disease remains prevalent.

Study Title: A Study of Ramucirumab Plus Pembrolizumab in Participants With Gastric or GEJ Adenocarcinoma, NSCLC, Transitional Cell Carcinoma of the Urothelium, or Biliary Tract Cancer
Indication/Condition: gastric adenocarcinoma, gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma, non-small cell lung cancer, transitional cell carcinoma of the urothelium, biliary tract cancer
Trial Summary: This Phase 1 trial evaluated the safety and preliminary efficacy of combining ramucirumab with pembrolizumab in 175 participants with various advanced cancers, including gastric or GEJ adenocarcinoma. The study's relevance lies in its exploration of a novel combination therapy approach, potentially offering new treatment avenues for difficult-to-treat cancers such as gastric cancer.

Study Title: ToGA Study - A Study of Herceptin (Trastuzumab) in Combination With Chemotherapy Compared With Chemotherapy Alone in Patients With HER2-Positive Advanced Gastric Cancer
Intervention(s): Trastuzumab vs Fluorouracil vs Cisplatin vs Capecitabine
Trial Summary: The ToGA Study is a completed Phase 3 trial that evaluated the impact of adding trastuzumab (Herceptin) to standard chemotherapy regimens in patients with HER2-positive advanced gastric cancer. Enrolling 584 participants, this study is significant as it explores the efficacy of targeting the HER2 protein, a novel approach in the treatment of gastric cancer, potentially offering improved survival outcomes for this patient population. The large sample size and the focus on a targeted therapy make it a pivotal study in the gastric cancer treatment landscape.

Study Title: Pembrolizumab/Placebo Plus Trastuzumab Plus Chemotherapy in Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 Positive (HER2+) Advanced Gastric or Gastroesophageal Junction (GEJ) Adenocarcinoma (MK-3475-811/KEYNOTE-811)
Trial Summary: The KEYNOTE-811 trial is a Phase 3 study evaluating the efficacy and safety of pembrolizumab combined with trastuzumab and standard chemotherapy versus trastuzumab with standard chemotherapy in patients with HER2-positive advanced gastric or gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma. With an enrollment of 738 participants, this trial is significant due to its large sample size and its potential to establish pembrolizumab as a superior treatment option, potentially improving progression-free and overall survival outcomes in this patient population.

Study Title: Phase II Trial of Neoadjuvant Pembrolizumab for Patients With Early Stage Gastroesophageal Adenocarcinoma
Trial Summary: This Phase II trial (NCT04089904) investigated the use of neoadjuvant pembrolizumab in patients with early-stage gastroesophageal adenocarcinoma, aiming to determine the pathologic complete response rate before surgical resection. Although the trial was terminated with only three participants enrolled, it remains of interest due to its exploration of pembrolizumab, an immunotherapy, in a neoadjuvant setting for gastric cancer, which could provide insights into potential new treatment strategies despite the limited data.

Study Title: A Study of Herceptin (Trastuzumab) in Combination With Cisplatin/Capecitabine Chemotherapy in Participants With Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2)-Positive Metastatic Gastric or Gastro-Esophageal Junction Cancer
Trial Summary: This Phase IIIb trial, now terminated, investigated the efficacy and safety of two dosing regimens of Herceptin (Trastuzumab) in combination with cisplatin and capecitabine chemotherapy for HER2-positive metastatic gastric or gastro-esophageal junction cancer. Despite its termination, the study remains relevant for the gastric cancer landscape as it explored alternative dosing strategies for Herceptin, a key therapeutic agent, potentially offering insights into optimizing treatment regimens for improved patient outcomes.
Endpoints: Progression-free Survival: median PFS 5.4 mo vs 4.4 mo, HR 1.13 (95% CI 0.63‑2.02)

Study Title: A Study of T-DXd for the Treatment of Solid Tumors Harboring HER2 Activating Mutations
Trial Summary: This Phase II trial investigates the efficacy and safety of Trastuzumab deruxtecan (T-DXd) in treating patients with unresectable or metastatic solid tumors that have specific HER2 activating mutations. With an enrollment of 102 participants, the study focuses on patients who have exhausted other treatment options, highlighting the potential of T-DXd as a novel therapeutic approach. This trial is particularly relevant for the gastric cancer landscape as it explores a targeted therapy for HER2 mutations, which could offer new treatment avenues for patients with limited options.
Endpoints: Progression-free Survival: median PFS 5.4 mo (95% CI 2.7‑7.1) by ICR, 4.4 mo (95% CI 2.8‑5.6) by Investigator assessment

Study Title: Study of Modified Docetaxel, Cisplatin, and Fluorouracil (mDCF) in Unresectable or Metastatic Gastric and Gastroesophageal Junction Adenocarcinoma
Trial Summary: The trial investigated a modified regimen of docetaxel, cisplatin, and fluorouracil (mDCF) with trastuzumab for patients with unresectable or metastatic gastric and gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma, aiming to reduce side effects while maintaining efficacy. This study is relevant to the gastric cancer landscape as it explores a potentially less toxic treatment option for Her2 positive tumors, which could improve patient quality of life and expand therapeutic strategies.

Study Title: Study of T-DXd Monotherapy in Patients With HER2-expressing Locally Advanced or Metastatic Gastric or GEJ Adenocarcinoma Who Have Received 2 or More Prior Regimens
Trial Summary: This Phase II trial evaluated the efficacy and safety of Trastuzumab Deruxtecan (T-DXd) as a monotherapy in 95 patients with HER2-expressing advanced gastric or gastroesophageal junction (GEJ) adenocarcinoma in China, who had previously undergone at least two treatment regimens. The study is significant for the gastric cancer landscape as it explores the potential of T-DXd, a novel antibody-drug conjugate, in a heavily pre-treated patient population, offering insights into new therapeutic options for this challenging condition.

Study Title: Safety and Efficacy of Pembrolizumab (MK-3475) in Combination With TS-1+Cisplatin or TS-1+Oxaliplatin as First Line Chemotherapy in Gastric Cancer (MK-3475-659/KEYNOTE-659)
Trial Summary: The trial investigated the safety and efficacy of pembrolizumab in combination with TS-1 and either cisplatin or oxaliplatin as a first-line treatment for PD-L1 positive, HER2-negative advanced gastric or gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma. With an enrollment of 100 participants, this Phase 2 study aimed to estimate the objective response rates of these combinations. This trial is relevant to the gastric cancer landscape as it explores the potential of pembrolizumab, an immune checkpoint inhibitor, in enhancing the effectiveness of standard chemotherapy regimens, offering insights into novel therapeutic strategies for this patient population.

Study Title: A Study to Test Combination Treatments in Participants With Advanced Gastric Cancer
Intervention(s): Nivolumab vs Ipilimumab vs Relatlimab vs BMS-986205 vs Rucaparib
Trial Summary: This Phase 2 trial, with an enrollment of 190 participants, evaluated the efficacy, safety, and tolerability of Nivolumab in combination with Ipilimumab and other therapies in individuals with advanced gastric cancer. The study is relevant to the gastric cancer landscape as it explores combination treatments, potentially offering new therapeutic options for a condition with limited effective treatments.

Study Title: A Combination Study of Kadcyla (Trastuzumab Emtansine) and Capecitabine in Participants With Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 (HER2)-Positive Metastatic Breast Cancer (mBC) or HER2-Positive Locally Advanced/Metastatic Gastric Cancer (LA/mGC)
Intervention(s): Trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1) + Capecitabine vs Trastuzumab emtansine (T-DM1)
Trial Summary: This Phase 2 trial, NCT01702558, aimed to evaluate the maximum tolerated dose and efficacy of combining Kadcyla (trastuzumab emtansine) with capecitabine in patients with HER2-positive metastatic breast cancer or locally advanced/metastatic gastric cancer. Although the trial was terminated, it remains of interest due to its exploration of a novel combination therapy targeting HER2-positive cancers, which could inform future research and treatment strategies in the gastric cancer landscape.
Endpoints: Overall survival: 'median OS 24.71 mo (lower limit 24.28 mo, upper limit NA)'

Study Title: T- XELOX in HER2-positive Stage III Gastric Cancer After D2 Gastrectomy
Trial Summary: This Phase 2 trial investigates the efficacy and safety of combining trastuzumab with XELOX in treating HER2-positive Stage III Gastric Cancer following D2 gastrectomy, enrolling 40 participants. The study is significant as it explores a targeted therapy approach in a specific gastric cancer subset, potentially offering improved outcomes for patients with HER2-positive tumors.

Study Title: A Study of Pembrolizumab (MK-3475) Versus Paclitaxel for Participants With Advanced Gastric/Gastroesophageal Junction Adenocarcinoma That Progressed After Therapy With Platinum and Fluoropyrimidine (MK-3475-061/KEYNOTE-061)
Trial Summary: This Phase 3 trial compared pembrolizumab to paclitaxel in 592 participants with advanced gastric or gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma that progressed after initial treatment with platinum and fluoropyrimidine. The study focused on patients with PD-L1 positive tumors, hypothesizing that pembrolizumab could improve progression-free and overall survival. This trial is significant in the gastric cancer landscape as it explores the efficacy of immunotherapy in a specific biomarker-defined population, potentially offering a targeted treatment option for patients with limited alternatives.
